sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
Now I can see the level 2 layout more clearly:
Left panel (gray background):
- 4 black buttons at row 33 (cols 7-9, 12-14, 17-19, 22-24) - these are the top buttons
- Below them are rows of white buttons at rows 35, 39, 41, 45, 47
Right panel (checkerboard):
- 2 yellow shapes (id=11 at row 7, id=44 at row 24)
- White buttons below (rows 33, 35, 39, 41, 45, 47)
Bottom area:
- Blue blob button (id=106) at (54, 42-50)
- Two colored panels at bottom left: blue (id=105) and gray (id=108)
- Yellow shapes in these panels: id=109 at (55,20-22) and id=110 at (57,8-9)
Looking at the image, the layout seems to be:
- Left panel: gray area with some shapes and buttons
- Right panel: checkerboard with 2 yellow shapes
- Below: two smaller preview panels (blue and gray) with yellow shapes inside them
- Blue blob button at bottom right
The key difference from level 1 is that there are now TWO panels side by side, and the buttons seem to be arranged differently. The left panel has black buttons on top and white buttons below, while the right panel has all white buttons.
Looking at the bottom panels:
- Blue panel (left): has a yellow shape inside
- Gray panel (right): has a yellow shape inside
I think the mechanic might be:
- Click the colored panel (blue or gray) to select it
- Then click buttons to configure something
- Then click the blue blob to submit
Or maybe the buttons need to be toggled to match some pattern. Let me look at what's different between the left and right panels.
Left panel buttons:
- Row 33: 4 black (B) buttons
- Rows 35, 39, 41, 45, 47: white (w) buttons
Right panel buttons:
- Row 33: 4 white (w) buttons  
- Rows 35, 39, 41, 45, 47: white (w) buttons
So the left panel has black top buttons and the right has white top buttons. The yellow shapes are only on the right panel.
Maybe I need to make the left panel match the right panel by clicking the black buttons to turn them white? Or maybe I need to click the blue/gray preview panels to select which panel to work on.
Let me try clicking the blue panel first to see what happens.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Let me look at the bottom preview panels more carefully
# Blue panel at (54,7-15), gray panel at (55,18-24)
# Let me check the ascii around these areas

# First, let me understand the structure better
# The bottom has two panels with yellow shapes inside
# Let me try clicking the blue panel to see what happens

# Blue panel center roughly at row 57, col 11
action([{'action': 'MOUSE', 'row': 57, 'col': 11}])

print(f"Board changed: {last_action_result.get('board_changed', False)}")
print(f"Level completed: {last_action_result.get('level_completed', False)}")

# Check what changed
if last_action_result.get('board_changed'):
    nodes_now = current_frame.segmentation['nodes']
    print(f"\nNodes after click: {len(nodes_now)}")
    # Check bottom area
    for n in nodes_now:
        if n['pixels'] < 100 and n['pixels'] > 1:
            b = n['boundary']
            min_row = min(p[0] for p in b)
            if min_row >= 50:
                min_col = min(p[1] for p in b)
                max_col = max(p[1] for p in b)
                print(f"  id={n['id']:3d} color={n['color']} pixels={n['pixels']:3d} at ({min_row},{min_col})-({max_col})")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>

sections.content:

  id=107 color=b pixels= 69 at (54,42)-(50)
  id=108 color=B pixels= 41 at (55,8)-(14)
  id=109 color=B pixels= 41 at (55,18)-(24)
  id=110 color=Y pixels=  6 at (55,20)-(22)
  id=111 color=Y pixels=  6 at (57,8)-(9)
